attributes.description: Alerts when the EC2 system status check fails, indicating underlying AWS host, hardware, or network impairment. Remediation is typically recover (stop/start on new hardware).
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] System status check failed

investigation_guide.blob:
## EC2 System Status Check Failed

### Meaning
The **system status check** validates AWS infrastructure beneath your instance (host hardware, hypervisor, physical network). A failure is an AWS-side problem, not a guest OS issue.

### Remediation
1. Wait briefly — transient AWS issues sometimes self-resolve.
2. If persistent, **recover** the instance (stop/start moves it to healthy hardware).
3. Open an AWS support case if recover does not resolve the issue.

### Do NOT
Reboot alone is unlikely to fix a system check failure — recover is the primary runbook action.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND MetricName == "StatusCheckFailed_System"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(InstanceId, " (", cloud.region, ")")
| STATS max_failed = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed_System`) BY ec2_instance
// 1 = failed — Maximum stat catches any failure within the period
| WHERE max_failed >= 1
| SORT max_failed DESC
| LIMIT 100
